behavior_summary: Provides item event handlers for a special key object: a simple on-U8P_KEYONEC::look handler that describes the key variant, and a complex on-use handler that can either perform a one-time interment ritual or fall back to alternate behavior (open keyring/other fallback).

On U8P_KEYONEC::look (KEYONEC::look) the script reads the key's frame and emits a short identifying text (bark) appropriate to that variant (e.g., 'Key of the Caretaker', 'Key of the Scion', 'Key of the Necromancer').

On use (KEYONEC::use) the script branches by the key's frame. For a particular frame (inferred frame==3) it checks a global guard (lothInterred). If not already set it validates the player target (inferred altar type), verifies reachability, scans nearby containers/tiles for required components (building a temporary list and counting variants), consumes/destroys chosen items, and runs a scripted, multi-stage interment ceremony: freezes the avatar (setAvatarInStasis), spawns timed/visual effects (FREE::*), animates NPCs (Npc::doAnim), plays sounds and camera quake, creates temporary sprites at world coordinates, updates the key's frame (Item::setFrame), and (inferred) sets lothInterred to mark completion.

If preconditions fail (lothInterred already set, wrong target, out of reach, or missing components) the use handler falls back to alternative behavior such as spawning KEYRING::0119 or printing short bark messages to inform the player.
notable_control_flow: KEYONEC::look: single branch based on Item::getFrame() selecting which bark string to emit (deterministic mapping of frames to name strings).
KEYONEC::use: early branch on Item::getFrame() to select ritual vs fallback paths.
In ritual path there is a guard read of global lothInterred that short-circuits ritual if already performed.
Target validation path: obtain process target(), check Item::getType() equals the expected altar type, then call Item::canReach() to ensure the player can reach the target.
Resource-gathering loop: scan nearby tiles/containers, append candidates to a temporary slist, maintain per-variant accumulators, then select and destroy required items.

Scripted sequence: sequence of visual/sound/camera/NPC animation calls executed in order with stasis set around them; side effects include spawn of multiple FREE::* processes and Item::setFrame() to update visuals.
open_questions: Exact numeric type id of the validated target is inferred (appears to be an altar) but not explicitly documented here; confirmation of type id (0x0274) is inferred.

Precise names, counts, and variant identities of the component items required by the ritual (how many of each frame/class must be present) are inferred from accumulation loops but not explicitly enumerated.

The explicit instruction that writes the global lothInterred flag is not shown in the excerpt but is inferred to occur at ritual completion; the exact write location and any additional side effects are not fully visible.
Behavior of spawned processes (KEYRING::0119, FREE::1C6D, FREE::1C19, FREE::1C8B) and the precise sprite ids/visual parameters used by createSprite are inferred by name but not fully specified.

main_function.name: U8P_KEYONEC::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_KEYONEC::look' (examine) event for the key item: read the item's frame and emit a short identifying message corresponding to the key variant.
entry_conditions: Event 0 / on-U8P_KEYONEC::look handler invoked when a player/actor examines the key item (enterFastArea semantics apply as area-trigger of a U8P_KEYONEC::look event).

function_map.name: U8P_KEYONEC::look
function_map.role: on-U8P_KEYONEC::look handler that reads Item::getFrame() and calls Item::bark() with a name string for the key variant

function_map.name: U8P_KEYONEC::use
function_map.role: on-use handler that performs reach/target validation, resource scanning/consumption, and a scripted interment ritual (or falls back to spawning KEYRING::0119 / showing barks)

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: query the item's frame/variant used to select behavior and text

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: emit a short text/dialog message associated with the item/process

function_map.name: U8P_KEYONEC::process target
function_map.role: retrieve the object the player targeted when using the item

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: return the target object's type id for validation (used to check for the altar)

function_map.name: spawn KEYRING::0119
function_map.role: fallback spawn when the ritual cannot proceed (inferred to open a keyring UI or alternate handling)

external_calls.purpose: Determine which key variant is being examined to choose the name string
Display the variant-specific short name/description to the player
Branch use behavior between ritual-capable variant(s) and fallback variants
Obtain the player's current target for validation (ensure the use is applied to the correct altar/object)
Verify the targeted object is the expected ritual altar/type before proceeding
Check reachability of the target to prevent out-of-range use
Animate NPC(s) involved in the ritual sequence
Temporarily freeze the player/avatar during the scripted ceremony
Spawn a visual/timed effect used during the interment sequence
Spawn an additional visual/timed effect during the ritual
Spawn an additional visual/timed effect during the ritual
Create a temporary ritual sprite at the key/altar coordinates for visual feedback
Update the key item's visual frame after parts of the ritual (reflecting consumed/changed state)
Play sound effects to emphasize key moments in the ceremony (quake, completion)
Begin a camera quake effect to dramatize the ritual
Stop the camera quake effect after the dramatic moment
Fallback behavior when ritual cannot proceed (lothInterred set or other early failures)
Query world coordinates to place effects and sprites at the key/altar location
Examine quantities of candidate items and consume/destroy chosen components as part of the ritual

reads.name: lothInterred
reads.likely_effect: Guard preventing re-running the Lothian interment ritual; read early in U8P_KEYONEC::use to take a fallback path when set

writes.name: lothInterred
writes.likely_effect: Set to mark that the interment ritual has completed successfully, preventing future repetitions of the ceremony (inferred from surrounding code and control flow)

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides item event handlers for a special key object: a simple on-U8P_KEYONEC::look handler that describes the key variant, and a complex on-use handler that can either perform a one-time interment ritual or fall back to alternate behavior (open keyring/other fallback).
